definition: Bandwidth is a range of frequencies, not raw throughput. Throughput is the actual data rate.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: Attenuation is signal weakening or degradation; noise is a common contributor.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: Frequency is cycles per second and is measured in hertz.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
Fiber is glass/plastic and can be fragile or require careful handling, even though it has low attenuation and excellent distance.
Wireless
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: CDPD is an older packet-data service for mobile Internet access. WAP is an application protocol, not the radio access service.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
Electric motors are common industrial EMI/noise sources.
Cabling
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
Encryption protects packet contents from being read by unauthorized parties.
Multiplexing
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
Asynchronous/statistical TDM dynamically assigns slots to active sources.
Modems & Access
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: Routers connect different IP networks and forward traffic to the Internet.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
All three are within RFC1918 private ranges; the PA expected 10.0.1.0/24 due single-answer format, but in real networking all three are valid private /24s.
Cabling
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
Policies define acceptable and unacceptable network use.
Error Detection
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition:
Start with needs before selecting hardware or implementation details.
Facilities
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: Broadband supports multiple channels/signals.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: Layer 2 handles framing and MAC addressing.
examTip: Study the concept, not just the answer letter.

definition: HTTP is an application-layer protocol, so HTTP errors are application-associated.
examTip: Application error often points to HTTP.

definition: DSL is a common broadband access technology that uses telephone copper infrastructure.
examTip: Common modem/access type points to DSL.

definition: A mesh topology connects nodes to one or more other nodes, often providing multiple paths.
examTip: Multiple host-to-host connections points to mesh.

definition: Each TV channel occupies a separate frequency range, which is frequency-division multiplexing.
examTip: If the scenario separates by frequency, choose FDM.

definition: Fixed recurring time slots are synchronous TDM.
examTip: Reserved slots even when idle means synchronous TDM.

definition: Statistical TDM dynamically assigns time slots to active sources.
CDMA separates users by unique codes rather than time or frequency.
examTip: Same time and frequency with codes means CDMA.

definition: CDMA systems use power control to manage the near-far problem.
examTip: Near-far issue points to CDMA power control.

definition: CDMA soft handoff maintains multiple tower connections during transition.
examTip: Multiple towers during transition means soft handoff.

definition: OFDM uses orthogonal subcarriers and performs well in multipath environments.
examTip: Multipath resistance and subcarriers point to OFDM.

definition: OFDM allows overlapping orthogonal subcarriers, unlike traditional FDM with guard bands.
WDM uses multiple wavelengths of light on one fiber.
examTip: Existing fiber plus multiple light channels means WDM.

definition: CWDM is lower-cost, uses wider spacing, and is common for shorter metro links.
DWDM uses densely spaced wavelengths for high-capacity long-haul fiber networks.
Optical amplifiers boost light directly without converting it back to electrical form.
examTip: No electrical conversion means optical amplifier.

definition: Single-mode fiber supports longer distances and lower modal dispersion than multimode fiber.
examTip: Long distance fiber usually means SMF.

definition: Multimode fiber has a larger core and is common for shorter runs.
Attenuation is signal loss or weakening over distance.
examTip: Weak signal over distance means attenuation.

definition: Dispersion spreads pulses over time, causing overlap and distortion.
An OTDR locates fiber faults by measuring reflected light.
Wi-Fi uses CSMA/CA because collision detection is impractical in wireless.
802.11n introduced MIMO and can use both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz.
802.11ax is Wi-Fi 6 and uses OFDMA.
Bluetooth is part of IEEE 802.15 wireless PAN standards.
2G introduced digital voice and SMS.
LTE is associated with 4G mobile broadband.
These features are associated with 5G networks.
examTip: Massive MIMO and low latency point to 5G.

definition: GSM is associated with SIM cards and TDMA-style access.
CRC is stronger than simple parity and is widely used for frame error detection.
examTip: Strong frame error detection points to CRC.

definition: Jitter buffers compensate for variation in packet arrival timing.
Routers forward packets between networks using IP information.
Switches use MAC address tables for LAN frame forwarding.
A repeater regenerates the signal at the physical layer.
RADIUS provides centralized authentication for network access, including enterprise Wi-Fi.
examTip: Enterprise Wi-Fi authentication points to RADIUS.

definition: DHCP automatically leases IP configuration to clients.
DNS resolves names to IP addresses.
192.168.0.0/16 is private IPv4 address space.
A baseline captures normal performance for future comparison.
QoS prioritizes time-sensitive traffic like voice and video.
Guest traffic should be isolated from internal trusted networks.
DSL uses telephone copper lines for broadband service.
DOCSIS defines data over cable service interface specifications for cable broadband.
examTip: Cable broadband over coax means DOCSIS.

definition: ISDN provides digital service with BRI and PRI interfaces.
SONET is a synchronous optical networking technology used by carriers.
Cat 6A supports 10GBASE-T to 100 meters.
RG-6 is commonly used for cable television and broadband coax drops.
examTip: Cable TV coax often means RG-6.

definition: RG-58 was used with 10BASE2 thin Ethernet.
Firewalls filter traffic based on policy.
Gateways connect unlike networks or translate protocols.
The Data Link layer handles frames and MAC addresses.
examTip: Frames and MACs mean Data Link.

definition: The Network layer handles routing and logical addressing such as IP.
The Transport layer manages TCP/UDP transport and ports.
The Presentation layer handles data format representation.
Modulation changes a carrier signal to encode information.
examTip: Carrier wave changed for data means modulation.

definition: Amplitude is the magnitude or height of a wave.
Frequency is cycles per second.
examTip: More cycles per second means frequency.

definition: Phase is a wave's position relative to a reference.
examTip: Relative position in cycle means phase.

definition: Parity uses an extra bit for odd/even checking.
Checksums compare calculated values to detect errors.
Channel planning reduces co-channel and adjacent-channel interference.
examTip: Overlapping Wi-Fi channels means channel planning.

definition: Encryption hides packet contents from unauthorized readers.
Network policies and procedures define expected and prohibited use.
examTip: Should/should not be used points to policies.

definition: Good network design plans for future growth and scalability.
CDPD provided cellular packet data service.
examTip: Older cellular packet data means CDPD.

definition: Beamforming steers signal energy toward users or directions.
Massive MIMO uses many antennas to improve capacity and performance.
examTip: Lots of antennas in 5G means massive MIMO.

definition: DWDM uses very tight spacing and supports many channels.
CWDM uses coarse 20 nm spacing and cheaper optics.
A multiplexer combines wavelengths, and a demultiplexer separates them.
A T1 circuit provides 1.544 Mbps service.
A T3 circuit provides about 44.736 Mbps.
APIPA assigns link-local 169.254.x.x addresses when DHCP fails.
NAT translates private internal addresses to public addresses.
A CSU/DSU terminates digital leased circuits such as T1.
